lever.summary: Detect terminal single-output F64 tensor Add DAGs with small tensors and replay the source-order DAG per element using dense VarId-indexed scalar slots, materializing only the final tensor.

isomorphism.ordering: Accepted DAGs process equations in source order for each tensor element.
isomorphism.tie_breaking: Ready-wave metadata is reconstructed by dependency level; visible intermediates, multi-output programs, nonterminal segments, params, effects, and sub-jaxprs fall back.
isomorphism.floating_point: F64 Add evaluation uses the original source order exactly, with no reassociation or element reordering.
isomorphism.storage: Dense F64 sources return dense F64 output; literal-backed sources return literal-backed output, preserving the visible storage class tested in the scheduler proof.
isomorphism.errors: Sparse VarIds, missing inputs, non-Add primitives, mismatched shapes or lengths, non-F64 tensors, short segments, and oversized tensors keep the existing path.

focused_tests.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-pass70-backend-focused2 cargo test -p fj-backend-cpu dependency_scheduler_direct -- --nocapture
